screen1.intro: Enter a URL and Veris runs an SEO + GEO diagnosis on real data (your GSC, measured answers from each AI engine, page crawls) — every conclusion comes with verifiable evidence.
screen1.enginesLabel: Probe engines (sources for measured GEO visibility)
screen1.gscDesc: Once connected, see your real rankings, impressions and clicks — the most accurate ground truth for diagnosis
screen1.submitError: Couldn't start the analysis. Please try again.
screen1.note: Sample interface. Diagnosis is asynchronous: each probe streams a result onto the next screen as it finishes, no idle waiting.

screen2.currentMeta: Each dimension shows a measured value only when backed by evidence; the rest are marked pending · open a measured card to view its raw evidence
screen2.pendingHint: This dimension depends on {source}; no real data source connected yet
screen2.uncollectedHint: The crawl source is ready; this run hasn't collected evidence for this dimension yet
screen2.mapPending: The answer presence map depends on AI probes, not connected yet
screen2.sovPending: Competitor Share of Voice depends on AI probes, not connected yet
screen2.emptyFindings: Page evidence collected this run; automatic finding generation is not connected yet
screen2.mapMeta: 20 high-intent prompts · teal = you appear / grey = you're absent
screen2.legendHover: Hover to see the exact prompt
screen2.findingsMeta: Expand any item to see the raw evidence
screen2.findingNotMentioned: In the sample probe for "project management tools for small teams," you're not mentioned while competitors Asana / Notion appear repeatedly
screen2.findingJsRender: Key landing page /features renders via JS; the non-rendered crawl path can't read its initial body text
screen2.findingLowCtr: 12 queries have impressions but abnormally low CTR; suspected SERP feature / AIO influence
screen2.findingBrandGood: For brand queries like "how is teamflow," the AI description is accurate and positive (already solid, just maintain)
screen2.note: Every measured number opens to its raw evidence; dimensions that depend on GSC / AI probes are not connected yet and are marked "pending" — never demo numbers dressed up as measured.

screen3.title: Recommendations
screen3.meta: Ranked by "impact × confidence ÷ effort" · each must be confirmed before output
screen3.empty: No recommendations yet this run; automatic finding-to-recommendation generation is not connected yet
screen3.recSsrTitle: Make /features server-rendered to reduce readability risk on the non-rendered crawl path
screen3.recSsrWhy: Some search and AI crawl paths don't execute full client-side JS. When content depends entirely on front-end rendering, the initial HTML lacks the core body text, which can lower the chance of being read, understood and cited.
screen3.recSsrEvidence: Crawling /features with JS off → 0 words of body text; 1,840 words after rendering. See evidence on the diagnosis page.
screen3.recSsrImpact: Puts core selling-point content into the initial HTML, raising the read probability for search and AI crawl paths; a high-impact, verifiable technical issue.
screen3.recContentTitle: Publish a "project management tools for small teams" comparison piece to earn AI citations
screen3.recContentEditNote: You're tuning the content angle and the brand facts to inject; once confirmed, a precise prompt is generated from this
screen3.recContentAngle: Answer-first + FAQ structure; a fair comparison of Asana/Notion/teamflow for "small team, limited budget, needs Gantt charts"; use real features and pricing, don't disparage competitors.
screen3.recContentFactsLabel: Injected brand facts (must not be fabricated)
screen3.recContentFacts: teamflow: free tier supports 10 users; dual board + Gantt views; Chinese docs and local support; pricing from ¥29/user/month.
screen3.recFaqTitle: Add FAQ schema and answer-first paragraphs to the 12 high-impression, low-CTR queries
screen3.recFaqWhy: These queries already have real impressions, rank 6–8 but with CTR under 1% — suspected SERP feature influence. Adding answer-first + FAQ may improve readability, snippet matching and click performance; verify via retest.
screen3.recFaqEvidence: GSC: "team task management software" impressions 8,420 / CTR 0.8% / rank 6.3. See the diagnosis page.
screen3.note: Human in the loop: the tool proposes, you decide. Only "accepted / edited" recommendations advance to prompt generation. Off-site recommendations (e.g. forums) only produce talking-point drafts, never auto-executed.

screen4.title: Output
screen4.meta: {count} confirmed · copy prompts on the left to execute, export the report on the right
screen4.emptyGated: No accepted / edited recommendations to generate execution prompts yet — confirm them on the Recommendations screen first
screen4.promptForContent: For recommendation · new small-team comparison content

promptTpl.system: You are a senior SEO + GEO expert. Execute the following confirmed optimization recommendation for {domain}:
promptTpl.brandFacts: [Brand facts you must use verbatim and never fabricate]

screen4.reportMeta: teamflow.cn · measured on 2026-06-29 · exportable as PDF / share link

report.snapshotBody: AI visibility 6/20; avg. organic rank 14.2; AI-crawlable pages 62%.
report.gapBody: Your SoV 30%, behind Asana (70%), Notion (55%), Monday (45%).
report.issuesBody: P1 landing page not AI-crawlable; P1 absent from all selection-intent prompts; P2 quick win on 12 high-impression, low-CTR queries. Each with evidence.
report.recsBody: 2 accepted, matching execution prompts generated; plan to fix crawlability first + 1 comparison piece, retest SoV in 4–6 weeks.
report.retestBody: Re-run the same 20 prompts in 6 weeks, measure the SoV delta, write into the living vertical playbook.

screen4.note: "Give the prompt" rather than "auto-publish": generation and publishing stay with a human + human review, keeping quality controllable and avoiding thin-content penalties. MCP-direct CMS writes can come later, but v1 stops at the precise prompt.
